description: Configuration for automated SLURM log publishing to CFS
_comment_overview: This configuration controls how SLURM job logs are published to the NERSC Science Gateway (CFS) for web access. The publisher scans harvester work directories, splits SLURM output by task/PandaID, and organizes files in a PandaID-centric structure.

paths._comment: All paths must be absolute. These are used by both the main script and parallel worker processes.
paths.workdir_root: <pathname to the harvester workdir, right above the panda queue sub-directories>
paths._comment_workdir_root: Root directory containing harvester work directories. Subdirectories are PanDA queue names (e.g., NERSC_Perlmutter_epic).
paths.cfs_destination: <pathname to the CFS directory, where panda queue sub-directories will be auto-created underneath>
paths._comment_cfs_destination: CFS destination for published logs. Web accessible at https://portal.nersc.gov/cfs/m3763/panda/jobs/. Files organized as <queue>/<pandaid>/
paths.state_file: <where do you want the .slurm_publish_state.json to be placed>
paths._comment_state_file: Global state file tracking processed jobs (old approach). Kept for migration compatibility and defense-in-depth.
paths._comment_log_file: Main log file for the publisher. Includes all INFO/WARNING/ERROR messages from main process and parallel workers.

timing._comment: Time-related settings for cleanup and job age checking.
timing._comment_retention_days: Number of days to keep published PandaID directories in CFS. Older directories are automatically deleted based on mtime.

processing._comment: Controls how SLURM jobs are processed and published.
processing._comment_split_script: Python script to split SLURM output by task/PandaID. Handles multiple PandaIDs per task.
processing._comment_max_concurrent_jobs: Maximum number of SLURM jobs to process in parallel. Lower for large files (3-5) to avoid I/O contention. Higher for small files (15-20) for faster processing. Implemented using Python multiprocessing.Pool.
processing._comment_delete_original_splits: Whether to delete split files (slurm-XXX-taskN-pandaXXX.out) from source after copying to CFS. Set false for debugging.

filters._comment: Criteria for which jobs to process.
filters._comment_ignore_queues: List of queue names to skip (e.g., ['test_queue', 'disabled_queue']).
filters._comment_only_completed_jobs: Only process jobs not in squeue (RUNNING/PENDING). Should always be true.
filters._comment_min_job_age_seconds: Minimum age (seconds) of slurm-<jobid>.out file before processing. Ensures SLURM has finished writing output.

additional_files_for_failed_tasks._comment: Task 28: For failed tasks (containing PanDA_Pilot-* subdirectory), copy additional files beyond pilotlog.txt. Each entry specifies glob patterns for source directory and files within that directory.
additional_files_for_failed_tasks._comment_src_dir: Glob pattern for source directory relative to task directory. Example: PanDA_Pilot-265737 matches PanDA_Pilot-*
additional_files_for_failed_tasks._comment_files: Comma-separated glob patterns for files within src_dir. Supports wildcards (e.g., 'payload.*', 'preprocess*.txt')

additional_files_for_failed_tasks.comment: Copy LOG subdirectory recursively for ePIC jobs
additional_files_for_failed_tasks._comment_src_dir_recursive: Can use multiple wildcards for nested paths. Example: PanDA_Pilot-265737/workDir/LOG
additional_files_for_failed_tasks._comment_files_recursive: Use '*' to copy all files in directory. Directory structure is preserved at destination.
